title: AD Group Audit and Local Admin Review
description: Use PowerShell to audit Domain Admins membership, identify service accounts by SPN, verify svc-historian's group memberships, and check local administrator group membership on PIPELINE-DC01.
context: You are logged into PIPELINE-DC01 as a domain administrator for ExampleCorp. A quarterly access review is underway. You need to verify Domain Admins group membership, identify service accounts, audit a specific service account, and check local administrator group membership.

steps.instruction: Enumerate all members of the Domain Admins group. List their Name and SamAccountName.
steps.hint: Use Get-ADGroupMember with -Identity 'Domain Admins' and pipe to Select-Object.

hintPatterns.hint: Close — use Get-ADGroupMember -Identity 'Domain Admins' to directly retrieve member objects rather than filtering Get-ADUser by group.
Get-LocalGroupMember retrieves local machine groups, not domain groups. Use Get-ADGroupMember for Active Directory groups.

steps.successOutput:
distinguishedName : CN=Administrator,CN=Users,DC=ExampleCorp,DC=local
name              : Administrator
objectClass       : user
objectGUID        : {guid}
SamAccountName    : Administrator
SID               : S-1-5-21-...

distinguishedName : CN=SVC-Pipeline-Backup,CN=ServiceAccounts,DC=ExampleCorp,DC=local
name              : SVC-Pipeline-Backup
objectClass       : user
objectGUID        : {guid}
SamAccountName    : svc-pipeline-backup
SID               : S-1-5-21-...
steps.feedbackOnWrong: Run: Get-ADGroupMember -Identity 'Domain Admins' | Select-Object Name, SamAccountName

steps.instruction: Find all Active Directory user accounts that have a ServicePrincipalName registered. These are your service accounts. Show Name, Enabled status, and ServicePrincipalName.
steps.hint: Use Get-ADUser with a -Filter that checks ServicePrincipalName, and include -Properties ServicePrincipalName.

hintPatterns.hint: Get-ADServiceAccount returns Managed Service Accounts (MSAs), not regular service accounts with SPNs. Use Get-ADUser -Filter {ServicePrincipalName -like '*'} -Properties ServicePrincipalName.
Close — add a filter: Get-ADUser -Filter {ServicePrincipalName -like '*'} to return only service accounts, and include -Properties ServicePrincipalName to see the registered SPNs.

steps.feedbackOnWrong: Run: Get-ADUser -Filter {ServicePrincipalName -like '*'} -Properties ServicePrincipalName | Select-Object Name, Enabled, ServicePrincipalName

steps.instruction: Check which Active Directory groups svc-historian belongs to. Verify it is not in any privileged groups.
steps.hint: Use Get-ADPrincipalGroupMembership with -Identity svc-historian and pipe to Select-Object Name.

hintPatterns.hint: Get-ADGroupMember lists members OF a group. To list groups that svc-historian belongs to, use Get-ADPrincipalGroupMembership -Identity svc-historian.
Close — Get-ADUser with -Properties MemberOf returns distinguished names. Use Get-ADPrincipalGroupMembership -Identity svc-historian | Select-Object Name for human-readable group names.

steps.successOutput:
distinguishedName : CN=Domain Users,CN=Users,DC=ExampleCorp,DC=local
GroupCategory     : Security
GroupScope        : Global
name              : Domain Users
objectClass       : group
objectGUID        : {guid}
SamAccountName    : Domain Users
SID               : S-1-5-21-...

distinguishedName : CN=Historian-DataReaders,OU=ServiceGroups,DC=ExampleCorp,DC=local
GroupCategory     : Security
GroupScope        : Global
name              : Historian-DataReaders
objectClass       : group
objectGUID        : {guid}
SamAccountName    : Historian-DataReaders
SID               : S-1-5-21-...

steps.instruction: Check the local Administrators group on this machine. Identify who has local admin rights.

hintPatterns.hint: net localgroup works but is not a PowerShell cmdlet. Use Get-LocalGroupMember -Group 'Administrators' for structured PS 5.1 output.
Get-ADGroupMember queries domain groups. For local machine groups like the local Administrators group, use Get-LocalGroupMember -Group 'Administrators'.

steps.successOutput:
ObjectClass Name                            PrincipalSource
----------- ----                            ---------------
User        PIPELINE-DC01\Administrator     Local
Group       EXAMPLECORP\Domain Admins        ActiveDirectory
User        EXAMPLECORP\svc-pipeline-backup  ActiveDirectory
